journal_entry.description: Major overhaul of machinery extending useful life
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS (IAS 16), subsequent expenditure on property, plant and equipment should be capitalized when it extends the useful life or enhances the asset's capacity. The $200,000 overhaul extends the remaining useful life by 2 years beyond the original estimate, meeting the capitalization criteria. The expenditure increases the carrying amount of the machinery asset and is paid in cash. No tax is applicable as this is a capital expenditure, not a purchase of goods or services subject to HST/GST.

output.reason: The $200,000 cash payment for the major overhaul represents an asset decrease (cash leaving the business). The overhaul is capitalized (added to the machinery asset account on the debit side) because it extends useful life, but on the credit side we only record the cash outflow. One credit line: cash decrease = asset decrease.

credit_classifier.reason: The $200,000 cash payment for the major overhaul represents an asset decrease (cash leaving the business). The overhaul is capitalized (added to the machinery asset account on the debit side) because it extends useful life, but on the credit side we only record the cash outflow. One credit line: cash decrease = asset decrease.

output.reason: The $200,000 major overhaul extends the useful life of the machinery, which under IFRS qualifies as a capital improvement that should be capitalized (added to the asset). This is 1 asset increase (debit to Machinery or similar fixed asset account). The original purchase is not part of this transaction - only the year 5 overhaul expenditure is being recorded here.

debit_classifier.reason: The $200,000 major overhaul extends the useful life of the machinery, which under IFRS qualifies as a capital improvement that should be capitalized (added to the asset). This is 1 asset increase (debit to Machinery or similar fixed asset account). The original purchase is not part of this transaction - only the year 5 overhaul expenditure is being recorded here.
